headline: Orang Asli Dam Resettlement Leaves 340 Families Without Clean Water
context: 340 Orang Asli families resettled for the Kelau Dam project in Pahang have been without reliable clean water since 2019, despite resettlement promises of piped supply.

cards.big: The Kelau Dam was built to provide clean water to Klang Valley.
cards.sub: 340 Orang Asli families displaced to provide that water have not had reliable clean water since resettlement.

cards.big: Resettlement houses were built without connection to the treated water network.
cards.sub: Residents rely on untreated stream water. Waterborne illness rates are 4x the state average.

cards.big: RM82M was allocated for the resettlement programme.
cards.sub: Audit found RM31M spent on road access and administration. RM8M on housing. RM0 on water connection.

cards.big: The dam supplies 1.1 billion litres daily to 4 million urban consumers.
cards.sub: 340 displaced families need 85,000 litres daily. The dam's output is 12,900 times greater than community need.

cards.big: A dam that provides water to 4 million people but not to the 340 families it displaced is development by extraction.
Displacement for infrastructure must meet minimum standards: equivalent or better services, monitored for 10 years. Anything less is state-sanctioned dispossession.
